  • What We Treat
    • What We Treat
    • Substance Use and Co-Occurring Disorders The uncontrolled use of drugs or alcohol despite negative consequences, often accompanied by another mental condition.
    • Anxiety Disorders The persistent, excessive and unrealistic worry about everyday things and situations that lasts at least six months.
    • Mood Disorders The elevation or lowering of mood to a degree that interferes with everyday life, including depression and bipolar disorder.
      • Depression Persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness and helplessness that interfere with thinking and daily activities.
      • Bipolar Disorder A multi-faceted mental illness that causes changes in a person's mood, energy and ability to function.
    • Personality Disorders Characterized by inflexibility of thought, intense emotions, erratic changes in mood, or harmful behaviors.
      • Borderline Personality Disorder A multi-faceted condition that can include instability in mood, self-image and personal relationships.
    • Trauma and Dissociative Disorders A treatment program designed for individuals with trauma and dissociation requiring an extended duration of care. This therapy-based program features a multimodal approach progressing from stabilization to processing to integration.
    • Psychotic Illnesses Characterized by an impaired relationship with reality, possibly including either hallucinations or delusions.
      • Schizoaffective Disorder The co-occurrence of reality distortion and mood disorder, with the features of reality distortion persisting.
      • Schizophrenia Characterized by a loss of appropriate emotional responses and the inability to organize thoughts and behaviors.
      • Bipolar Disorder Characterized by changes in a person’s mood, energy and ability to function.

  • How We Treat
    • How We Treat
    • Co-Occurring Disorders When two or more illnesses occur at the same time in a patient, including substance use disorder.
    • Dialectical Behavior Therapy A proven therapy for a variety of mental health and substance use disorders that builds skills to regulate emotions, control behaviors and improve relationships.
    • Comprehensive Psychiatric Evaluation An extensive assessment program to help individuals gain a comprehensive and deep understanding of their whole self and a plan and recommendation for moving forward.
    • Cognitive Behavioral Therapy A psychotherapy that identifies problematic thought patterns and helps patients understand how those thoughts affect their feelings and behaviors.
    • Additional Therapeutic Approaches Behavioral, medical and skill building therapies that increase options for successful treatment.
    • Alternative and Complementary Therapies A variety of medical and creative therapies designed to reinforce and support a patient’s treatment program.
    • Levels of Care Patients move seamlessly between levels of treatment depending upon their need.
      • Inpatient The most intensive form of care, indicated for a phase of illness that requires a great deal of management.
      • Residential Treatment on an extended basis for patients with stable symptoms who are proceeding with recovery.
      • Outpatient Patients living at home while continuing treatment and skills development at Silver Hill.

No Place Like Scavetta
Reclaiming Lives: Silver Hill's Substance Use and Co-Occurring Disorders Program

There was no easing into 2026 at the DBT-S Residential Program at Scavetta House this January. “At most times this winter, there’s been a foot or two of snow outside and a warm full house inside,” says Ryan Wade, MD, Director of the Program, “Just as we like it.”

 

The 30-day residential program (living room pictured above) serves up to 16 patients with a full daily schedule of individual and group therapies, medication management, recovery meetings, experiential and creative therapies, and time for gym, meals, and connection. The quality treatment, environment, and support are life changing.  See Program Details >

Out of Network
Residential Program 

We provide assistance to help patients navigate their out-of-network insurance benefits for our residential programs, making the process easier and more accessible. 

Learn More
Treatment for Adults
When addiction has an additional component, recovery can be challenging

While you may be seeking treatment for addiction, we find that there is often a psychiatric disorder that occurs along with it such as anxiety, depression or bipolar disorder. Our treatment team has the expertise and experience to treat even the most complex presentations. 

Learn More
For Clinicians
Do you have a patient that could benefit from an environment of care?

From initial diagnosis through return to your care, we are here to partner with you in the successful treatment of your patients. Silver Hill Hospital is renowned for its clinical expertise, proven treatments and nurturing support. See what some of our former patients along with their families have to say about their time at Silver Hill Hospital.

Read Patient Testimonials
The Steward House at Silver Hill
A refuge of healing and recovery exclusively for executives and professionals

The Steward House brings together the collective clinical strength and longstanding track record of excellence of venerable institutions, Silver Hill Hospital and Yale New Haven Health and Yale Medicine. Our premier living program is designed exclusively for professional leaders and executives—the stewards of their organization—who are struggling with serious mental health or substance use problems. Our state-of-the-art program allows patients to undergo the intensive therapy necessary for recovery in a private, safe environment.

Learn more
Financial Assistance
Financial assistance is available at Silver Hill Hospital

Silver Hill Hospital was founded on, and committed to the principle of helping others. Our long-standing financial assistance fund ensures our services, Inpatient Care and our Residential Programs are made available to those who meet the clinical and financial criteria to receive treatment.

Learn more

 What We Treat 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 What We Treat 
 

 

 

 

 
 Substance Use and Co-Occurring Disorders
 
 The uncontrolled use of drugs or alcohol despite negative consequences, often accompanied by another mental condition. 
 

 

 

 

 
 Anxiety Disorders
 
 The persistent, excessive and unrealistic worry about everyday things and situations that lasts at least six months. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Mood Disorders 
 The elevation or lowering of mood to a degree that interferes with everyday life, including depression and bipolar disorder. 
 

 
 
 
 Depression
 
 Persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness and helplessness that interfere with thinking and daily activities. 
 

 
 
 Bipolar Disorder
 
 A multi-faceted mental illness that causes changes in a person's mood, energy and ability to function. 
 

 
 

 

 

 Personality Disorders 
 Characterized by inflexibility of thought, intense emotions, erratic changes in mood, or harmful behaviors. 
 

 
 
 
 Borderline Personality Disorder
 
 A multi-faceted condition that can include instability in mood, self-image and personal relationships. 
 

 
 

 

 

 
 Trauma and Dissociative Disorders
 
 A treatment program designed for individuals with trauma and dissociation requiring an extended duration of care. This therapy-based program features a multimodal approach progressing from stabilization to processing to integration. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Psychotic Illnesses 
 Characterized by an impaired relationship with reality, possibly including either hallucinations or delusions. 
 

 
 
 
 Schizoaffective Disorder
 
 The co-occurrence of reality distortion and mood disorder, with the features of reality distortion persisting. 
 

 
 
 Schizophrenia
 
 Characterized by a loss of appropriate emotional responses and the inability to organize thoughts and behaviors. 
 

 
 
 Bipolar Disorder
 
 Characterized by changes in a person’s mood, energy and ability to function.

 Comprehensive Psychiatric Evaluation 
 An extensive assessment program to help individuals gain a comprehensive and deep understanding of their whole self and a plan and recommendation for moving forward. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Cognitive Behavioral Therapy 
 A psychotherapy that identifies problematic thought patterns and helps patients understand how those thoughts affect their feelings and behaviors. 
 

 

 

 

 Additional Therapeutic Approaches 
 Behavioral, medical and skill building therapies that increase options for successful treatment. 
 

 

 

 

 Alternative and Complementary Therapies 
 A variety of medical and creative therapies designed to reinforce and support a patient’s treatment program. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Levels of Care 
 Patients move seamlessly between levels of treatment depending upon their need. 
 

 
 
 
 Inpatient
 
 The most intensive form of care, indicated for a phase of illness that requires a great deal of management. 
 

 
 
 Residential
 
 Treatment on an extended basis for patients with stable symptoms who are proceeding with recovery.

 Patients living at home while continuing treatment and skills development at Silver Hill.

 What We Treat 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 What We Treat 
 

 

 

 

 
 Substance Use and Co-Occurring Disorders
 
 The uncontrolled use of drugs or alcohol despite negative consequences, often accompanied by another mental condition. 
 

 

 

 

 
 Anxiety Disorders
 
 The persistent, excessive and unrealistic worry about everyday things and situations that lasts at least six months. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Mood Disorders 
 The elevation or lowering of mood to a degree that interferes with everyday life, including depression and bipolar disorder. 
 

 
 
 
 Depression
 
 Persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness and helplessness that interfere with thinking and daily activities. 
 

 
 
 Bipolar Disorder
 
 A multi-faceted mental illness that causes changes in a person's mood, energy and ability to function. 
 

 
 

 

 

 Personality Disorders 
 Characterized by inflexibility of thought, intense emotions, erratic changes in mood, or harmful behaviors. 
 

 
 
 
 Borderline Personality Disorder
 
 A multi-faceted condition that can include instability in mood, self-image and personal relationships. 
 

 
 

 

 

 
 Trauma and Dissociative Disorders
 
 A treatment program designed for individuals with trauma and dissociation requiring an extended duration of care. This therapy-based program features a multimodal approach progressing from stabilization to processing to integration. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Psychotic Illnesses 
 Characterized by an impaired relationship with reality, possibly including either hallucinations or delusions. 
 

 
 
 
 Schizoaffective Disorder
 
 The co-occurrence of reality distortion and mood disorder, with the features of reality distortion persisting. 
 

 
 
 Schizophrenia
 
 Characterized by a loss of appropriate emotional responses and the inability to organize thoughts and behaviors. 
 

 
 
 Bipolar Disorder
 
 Characterized by changes in a person’s mood, energy and ability to function. 
 

 
 

 

 

 All Conditions

 How We Treat 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 How We Treat 
 

 

 

 

 Co-Occurring Disorders 
 When two or more illnesses occur at the same time in a patient, including substance use disorder. 
 

 

 

 

 Dialectical Behavior Therapy 
 A proven therapy for a variety of mental health and substance use disorders that builds skills to regulate emotions, control behaviors and improve relationships. 
 

 

 

 

 Comprehensive Psychiatric Evaluation 
 An extensive assessment program to help individuals gain a comprehensive and deep understanding of their whole self and a plan and recommendation for moving forward. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Cognitive Behavioral Therapy 
 A psychotherapy that identifies problematic thought patterns and helps patients understand how those thoughts affect their feelings and behaviors. 
 

 

 

 

 Additional Therapeutic Approaches 
 Behavioral, medical and skill building therapies that increase options for successful treatment. 
 

 

 

 

 Alternative and Complementary Therapies 
 A variety of medical and creative therapies designed to reinforce and support a patient’s treatment program. 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 Levels of Care 
 Patients move seamlessly between levels of treatment depending upon their need. 
 

 
 
 
 Inpatient
 
 The most intensive form of care, indicated for a phase of illness that requires a great deal of management. 
 

 
 
 Residential
 
 Treatment on an extended basis for patients with stable symptoms who are proceeding with recovery. 
 

 
 
 Outpatient
 
 Patients living at home while continuing treatment and skills development at Silver Hill.

  • What We Treat
    • What We Treat
    • Substance Use and Co-Occurring Disorders The uncontrolled use of drugs or alcohol despite negative consequences, often accompanied by another mental condition.
    • Anxiety Disorders The persistent, excessive and unrealistic worry about everyday things and situations that lasts at least six months.
    • Mood Disorders The elevation or lowering of mood to a degree that interferes with everyday life, including depression and bipolar disorder.
      • Depression Persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness and helplessness that interfere with thinking and daily activities.
      • Bipolar Disorder A multi-faceted mental illness that causes changes in a person's mood, energy and ability to function.
    • Personality Disorders Characterized by inflexibility of thought, intense emotions, erratic changes in mood, or harmful behaviors.
      • Borderline Personality Disorder A multi-faceted condition that can include instability in mood, self-image and personal relationships.
    • Trauma and Dissociative Disorders A treatment program designed for individuals with trauma and dissociation requiring an extended duration of care. This therapy-based program features a multimodal approach progressing from stabilization to processing to integration.
    • Psychotic Illnesses Characterized by an impaired relationship with reality, possibly including either hallucinations or delusions.
      • Schizoaffective Disorder The co-occurrence of reality distortion and mood disorder, with the features of reality distortion persisting.
      • Schizophrenia Characterized by a loss of appropriate emotional responses and the inability to organize thoughts and behaviors.
      • Bipolar Disorder Characterized by changes in a person’s mood, energy and ability to function.
    • All Conditions

  • How We Treat
    • How We Treat
    • Co-Occurring Disorders When two or more illnesses occur at the same time in a patient, including substance use disorder.
    • Dialectical Behavior Therapy A proven therapy for a variety of mental health and substance use disorders that builds skills to regulate emotions, control behaviors and improve relationships.
    • Comprehensive Psychiatric Evaluation An extensive assessment program to help individuals gain a comprehensive and deep understanding of their whole self and a plan and recommendation for moving forward.
    • Cognitive Behavioral Therapy A psychotherapy that identifies problematic thought patterns and helps patients understand how those thoughts affect their feelings and behaviors.
    • Additional Therapeutic Approaches Behavioral, medical and skill building therapies that increase options for successful treatment.
    • Alternative and Complementary Therapies A variety of medical and creative therapies designed to reinforce and support a patient’s treatment program.
    • Levels of Care Patients move seamlessly between levels of treatment depending upon their need.
      • Inpatient The most intensive form of care, indicated for a phase of illness that requires a great deal of management.
      • Residential Treatment on an extended basis for patients with stable symptoms who are proceeding with recovery.
      • Outpatient Patients living at home while continuing treatment and skills development at Silver Hill.

rawMentions: Residential, Inpatient, Outpatient Care | Silver Hill Hospital
There was no easing into 2026 at the DBT-S Residential Program at Scavetta House this January.

The 30-day residential program (living room pictured above) serves up to 16 patients with a full daily schedule of individual and group therapies, medication management, recovery meetings, experiential and creative therapies, and time for gym, meals, and connection.
We provide assistance to help patients navigate their out-of-network insurance benefits for our residential programs, making the process easier and more accessible.

Our long-standing financial assistance fund ensures our services, Inpatient Care and our Residential Programs are made available to those who meet the clinical and financial criteria to receive treatment.
Silver Hill offers three levels of care: inpatient, residential and intensive outpatient treatment.
